```{r}
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(moddep1MedM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc3m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Medium Workload of Any Depression")
```
mean ICC 0.001964791
no outlier
scattered

new mean ICC 0.002004233
new plot not significant outlier

0.002004226

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with high data for any depression
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_HM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  moddep1HM1 <- glmer(depnew1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(moddep1HM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc3m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for High Workload of Any Depression")
```
mean ICC 0.0006376662
outlier

new mean ICC 0.000759813
new plot few outliers

0.000759801

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with very high data for any depression
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsVHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_VHM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  moddep1VHM1 <- glmer(depnew1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(moddep1VHM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc3m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Depression")
```
mean ICC 3.922692e-14
one outlier
scattered 

new mean ICC 7.86619e-14
new plot one outlier

4.291336e-14


#WITH EXERCISE

#FOR LOW WORKLOAD
```{r}
modex1LowM <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_lowM)

summary(modex1LowM)
#sign
#
modex1LowMN <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_lowM)

summary(modex1LowMN)
```

#icc for Low
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance1 <- VarCorr(modex1LowM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m <- random_intercept_variance1 / (random_intercept_variance1 + pi^2 / 3)
icc1m
#0.07
#
random_intercept_variance1N <- VarCorr(modex1LowMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1mN <- random_intercept_variance1N / (random_intercept_variance1N + pi^2 / 3)
icc1mN
```

#FOR MEDIUM WORKLOAD
```{r}
modex1MedM <- glmer(exer1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_MedM)

summary(modex1MedM)
#sign
#
modex1MedMN <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_MedM)

summary(modex1MedMN)
```

#icc for MEDIUM
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance2 <- VarCorr(modex1MedM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m <- random_intercept_variance2 / (random_intercept_variance2 + pi^2 / 3)
icc2m
#0
#
random_intercept_variance2N <- VarCorr(modex1MedMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2mN <- random_intercept_variance2N / (random_intercept_variance2N + pi^2 / 3)
icc2mN
```

#FOR HIGH WORKLOAD
```{r}
modex1HM <- glmer(exer1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                 weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_HM)

summary(modex1HM)
#intercept sig
#
#
modex1HMN <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                 weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_HM)

summary(modex1HMN)
```

#icc for HIGH
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance3 <- VarCorr(modex1HM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3m <- random_intercept_variance3 / (random_intercept_variance3 + pi^2 / 3)
icc3m
#0.07
#
random_intercept_variance3N <- VarCorr(modex1HMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3mN <- random_intercept_variance3N / (random_intercept_variance3N + pi^2 / 3)
icc3mN
```


#FOR VERY HIGH WORKLOAD
```{r}
modex1VHM <- glmer(exer1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                  weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_VHM)

summary(modex1VHM)
#intercept sig
#
modex1VHMN <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                  weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_VHM)

summary(modex1VHMN)
```

#icc for VERY HIGH
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance4 <- VarCorr(modex1VHM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc4m <- random_intercept_variance4 / (random_intercept_variance4 + pi^2 / 3)
icc4m
#0.04
#
random_intercept_variance4N <- VarCorr(modex1VHMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc4mN <- random_intercept_variance4N / (random_intercept_variance4N + pi^2 / 3)
icc4mN
```

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with low data for any exercise
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsLM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_lowM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modgen1LowM1 <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modgen1LowM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc2m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Low Workload of Any Exercise")
```
mean ICC 0.06032196
no outlier

new mean ICC 0.05830256
new plot some outliers

0.05830255

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with medium data for any exercise
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsMM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_MedM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modgen1MedM1 <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modgen1MedM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc2m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Medium Workload of Any Exercise")
```
mean ICC 0.004362907
one outlier but not significant

new mean ICC 0.004595576
new plot few outliers

0.004595571

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with high data for any exercise
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_HM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modgen1HM1 <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modgen1HM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc2m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for High Workload of Any Exercise")
```
mean ICC 0.06589049
couple of outliers 

new mean ICC 0.06598516
new plot outliers

0.06598515

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with very high data for any exercise
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsVHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_VHM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modgen1VHM1 <- glmer(exernew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modgen1VHM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc2m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Exercise")
```
mean ICC 0.04245941
few outliers

new mean ICC 0.04252395
new plot few outliers

0.04252395

#FOR HEALTH COVERAGE

#FOR LOW WORKLOAD
```{r}
modhcovLowM <- glmer(hcov ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                    weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_lowM)

summary(modhcovLowM)


modhcovLowMN <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                    weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_lowM)

summary(modhcovLowMN)

```

#icc for Low
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance1 <- VarCorr(modhcovLowM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m <- random_intercept_variance1 / (random_intercept_variance1 + pi^2 / 3)
icc1m
#0.0
#
#
random_intercept_variance1N <- VarCorr(modhcovLowMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1mN <- random_intercept_variance1N / (random_intercept_variance1N + pi^2 / 3)
icc1mN
#0
```

#FOR MEDIUM WORKLOAD
```{r}
modhcovMedM <- glmer(hlthcov1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                    weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_MedM)

summary(modhcovMedM)

modhcovMedMN <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                    weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_MedM)

summary(modhcovMedMN)

```

#icc for MEDIUM
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance2 <- VarCorr(modhcovMedM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2m <- random_intercept_variance2 / (random_intercept_variance2 + pi^2 / 3)
icc2m
#0.0
#
random_intercept_variance2N <- VarCorr(modhcovMedMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc2mN <- random_intercept_variance2N / (random_intercept_variance2N + pi^2 / 3)
icc2mN
#0
```

#FOR HIGH WORKLOAD
```{r}
modhcovHM <- glmer(hlthcov1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                  weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_HM)

summary(modhcovHM)
#intercept sig
#
#
modhcovHMN <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                  weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_HM)

summary(modhcovHMN)
```

#icc for HIGH
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance3 <- VarCorr(modhcovHM)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3 <- random_intercept_variance3 / (random_intercept_variance3 + pi^2 / 3)
icc3
#0.01
#
random_intercept_variance3N <- VarCorr(modhcovHMN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc3N <- random_intercept_variance3N / (random_intercept_variance3N + pi^2 / 3)
icc3N
#0.01
```


#FOR VERY HIGH WORKLOAD
```{r}
modhcovVH <- glmer(hlthcov1 ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_VHWL)

summary(modhcovVH)
#intercept sig
#
modhcovVHN <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = dat12c_VHWL)

summary(modhcovVHN)
```

#icc for VERY HIGH
```{r}
# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance4 <- VarCorr(modhcovVH)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc4 <- random_intercept_variance4 / (random_intercept_variance4 + pi^2 / 3)
icc4
#0.08
#
random_intercept_variance4N <- VarCorr(modhcovVHN)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc4N <- random_intercept_variance4N / (random_intercept_variance4N + pi^2 / 3)
icc4N
#0.08
```


#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with low data for any health coverage
```{r}
#dat12c_lowM$intvid_state <- paste(dat12c_lowM$intvid, dat12c_lowM$state, sep = "_")

# Identify unique interviewers including state information
interviewers1 <- unique_countsLM$intvid

# Identify unique interviewers
#interviewers <- unique(dat12c_lowM$intvid)

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_lowM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modhcov1LowM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modhcov1LowM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc1m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Low Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```
mean ICC 0.018995
one outlier 

new mean icc 0.01582917
new plot 6 outliers

0.01582917

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with medium data for any health coverage
```{r}
#dat12c_MedM$intvid_state1 <- paste(dat12c_MedM$intvid, dat12c_MedM$state, sep = "_")
# Identify unique interviewers
#interviewers <- unique(dat12c_MedM$intvid)
#
# Identify unique interviewers including state information
interviewers1 <- unique_countsMM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_MedM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modhcov1MedM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modhcov1MedM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc1m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Medium Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```
mean ICC 7.746634e-16
one outlier 

new mean icc 5.620107e-16
new plot few outliers

8.316439e-16

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with high data for any health coverage
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_HM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modhcov1HM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modhcov1HM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc1m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for High Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```
mean ICC 0.01417939
one outlier

new mean icc 0.01427636
new plot few outliers

0.01427636

#Chekcing ICC variation for Dec with very high data for any health coverage
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsVHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_VHM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modhcov1VHM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modhcov1VHM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc1m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```
mean ICC 0.09016529
few outliers

new mean icc 0.09023645
new plot some outliers

0.09023649


#removing intvids
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers1 <- unique_countsVHM$intvid

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers1)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_VHM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers1[i])
  
  modhcov1VHM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41 <- VarCorr(modhcov1VHM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1 <- random_intercept_variance41 / (random_intercept_variance41 + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list[i] <- icc1m1
}

mean(coefficients_list)

# Filter coefficients_list to exclude values less than or equal to 0.088
filtered_coefficients <- coefficients_list[coefficients_list > 0.088]

# Calculate the mean of the filtered coefficients
mean_filtered <- mean(filtered_coefficients)

# Display the mean of the filtered coefficients
print(mean_filtered)

plot(filtered_coefficients, ylim=c(0,0.15) ,xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage filtered")
```
0.09037814


#trying to remove interviewers
```{r}
# Identify unique interviewers
interviewers2 <- unique_countsVHM$intvid[-c(192, 193, 263, 270, 270, 535, 536, 537, 869, 870, 871, 893, 895, 896, 1028, 1029, 1030, 1190, 1191)]

# Create an empty list to store coefficients
coefficients_list1 <- c()

# Loop through each interviewer
for (i in 1:length(interviewers2)) {
  # Subset data excluding the current interviewer
  subset_data <- dat12c_VHM %>% filter(intvid != interviewers2[i])
  
  modhcov1VHM1 <- glmer(hcovnew ~ 1 + (1 | intvid),
                   weights = wt3>0,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"),
  nAGQ = 1,
  data = subset_data)

# Extract variance of the random intercept from the model
random_intercept_variance41a <- VarCorr(modhcov1VHM1)$intvid[1]

# Calculate ICC
icc1m1a <- random_intercept_variance41a / (random_intercept_variance41a + pi^2 / 3)

  
  # Store coefficients in the list
  coefficients_list1[i] <- icc1m1a
}

mean(coefficients_list1)

plot(coefficients_list1, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylim=c(0,0.1), ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage after removing ints")

#number of ints of the outliers
outlierints <- which(coefficients_list1<=0.086)

#icc for <0.086
coefficients_list[which(coefficients_list<=0.086)]

# Filter coefficients_list to exclude values less than or equal to 0.088
filtered_coefficients <- coefficients_list[coefficients_list > 0.088]

plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```

0.09031028


```{r}
unique_countsLM <- dat12c_lowM %>% 
  group_by(intvid, state) %>% 
  summarise(count = n())

# Count the number of unique interviewers
num_unique_interviewersLM <- nrow(unique_countsLM)
num_unique_interviewersLM
```
36

```{r}
unique_countsMM <- dat12c_MedM %>% 
  group_by(intvid, state) %>% 
  summarise(count = n())

# Count the number of unique interviewers
num_unique_interviewersMM <- nrow(unique_countsMM)
num_unique_interviewersMM
```
153

```{r}
unique_countsHM <- dat12c_HM %>% 
  group_by(intvid, state) %>% 
  summarise(count = n())

# Count the number of unique interviewers
num_unique_interviewersHM <- nrow(unique_countsHM)
num_unique_interviewersHM
```
504

```{r}
unique_countsVHM <- dat12c_VHM %>% 
  group_by(intvid, state) %>% 
  summarise(count = n())

# Count the number of unique interviewers
num_unique_interviewersVHM <- nrow(unique_countsVHM)
num_unique_interviewersVHM
```
1726

```{r}
unique_countsM <- dat12cM %>% 
  group_by(intvid, state) %>% 
  summarise(count = n())

# Count the number of unique interviewers
num_unique_interviewersM <- nrow(unique_countsM)
num_unique_interviewersM
```
2419

```{r}
# par(mfrow=c(1,3))
# genVH
# plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage")
# plot(coefficients_list, xlab = "Interviewer ID", ylab = "ICC for Very High Workload of Any Health Coverage")
```

```{r}
dat12c_hd$depnew <- as.numeric(dat12c_hd$depr1)
table(dat12c_hd$depnew)
```
